retry_reason: If dev is non-NULL, the refcount decrement is unconditional and always succeeds; retrying the same function on the same pointer after it reached zero would be a double-put (use-after-free). If dev is NULL, it's a no-op that will never change state.
contract_summary: Decrements the reference count of a net_device. If the per-CPU refcount config is enabled, uses a per-CPU decrement; otherwise uses an atomic refcount_dec. The caller must not access the device after the last reference is dropped, as it may be freed. This function is a standard put wrapper: it releases one reference that was previously acquired via a get operation. No return value is given—the caller does not need to check anything.
